steps.title: Read the vertex form
steps.content: The function is written as $f(x)=(x-2)^2-4$. Since the squared term is never negative, the smallest value happens when the squared term equals $0$.

steps.title: Find the minimum value
steps.content:
Set the squared part to $0$.
$$(x-2)^2=0$$
$$f(x)=0-4=-4$$
The answer is A.
